name: Smooth Follow Camera
description: A camera behavior that follows a target with damping, lookahead, dead zones, or catch-up rules instead of hard snapping.
design_notes: Smooth follow improves readability when tuned around the game's movement speed, screen size, and hazards. The camera should feel calm without lagging behind important threats. Teleports, respawns, room transitions, and fast traversal need explicit snap or catch-up rules.
design_purpose: Keep the player and nearby threats framed without abrupt camera jumps.
Use lookahead or dead zones to show more space in the direction of intent.
Reduce visual noise while preserving control responsiveness.
player_fantasy: The player feels the camera is a steady partner that anticipates movement without fighting input.

parameters.name: follow_half_life_ms
parameters.description: Damping half-life used when closing distance to the target.

parameters.name: lookahead_distance
parameters.description: Additional framing offset in movement or aim direction.

parameters.name: dead_zone_width_percent
parameters.description: Horizontal area the target can move within before camera follows.

parameters.name: dead_zone_height_percent
parameters.description: Vertical area the target can move within before camera follows.

parameters.name: teleport_snap_distance
parameters.description: Target displacement that snaps the camera instead of smoothing.

edge_cases: Respawn, fast travel, teleport blink, or room transitions should snap or blend from a known state.
Camera bounds can conflict with lookahead near level edges and reveal empty space or hide hazards.
Moving platforms and elevators need vertical follow tuning that avoids seasick lag.
Split-screen cameras need independent smoothing so one player does not inherit another player's velocity.
common_bugs: Damping uses frame-based interpolation and feels different at 30 FPS and 144 FPS.
Lookahead changes direction instantly on input reversal and causes camera wobble.
Camera remains outside level bounds after a teleport because bounds are applied before snapping.
Dead zone math uses pixels in one place and normalized screen units in another.
balancing_notes: More smoothing feels cinematic but can hide sudden hazards in fast platformers.
Lookahead should support intended movement direction without showing offscreen secrets too early.
accessibility_notes: Expose camera smoothing and lookahead intensity options when camera lag can affect comfort or precision.
Avoid oscillating follow behavior; stable camera motion helps players with motion sensitivity and spatial tracking needs.

implementation_notes.unity: Use Cinemachine damping/dead-zone settings or a custom exponential smoothing model with delta-time independent half-life values.
implementation_notes.godot: For Camera2D, combine smoothing with drag margins or custom dead-zone logic; for 3D rigs, smooth the pivot target before collision checks.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use SpringArm lag or PlayerCameraManager smoothing, but snap on teleports and reset interpolation state when camera modes change.
implementation_notes.web: Implement smoothing with exponential decay using delta time, apply dead-zone correction in world units, and clamp the result to level bounds last.

relationships.reason: Smooth follow damping is usually tuned around the default walk and run acceleration profile.
Fast attachment and release movement can outrun camera damping unless catch-up and lookahead rules are tuned.
Camera assist settings can adjust follow damping, recenter delay, or lookahead for comfort.

example_games.title: Celeste
example_games.note: Uses controlled camera following to support precision platforming readability.

example_games.title: Hollow Knight
example_games.note: Uses smooth camera movement and room framing to support exploration and combat.

example_games.title: Super Mario Odyssey
example_games.note: Uses smooth camera following and framing to support 3D platforming readability.
